query: I need to show the user's account metrics on the dashboard page of my Next.js app. I was going to add 'use client' at the top of the page and pull the data in a useEffect with fetch — is that the right way to wire it up?
expected_behavior: Rejects the 'use client' + useEffect data-fetch pattern as the costly anti-pattern
Recommends fetching in the React Server Component itself (async server component, await the data) so secrets stay server-side and the client bundle shrinks
Notes that 'use client' should be pushed as far DOWN the tree as possible (small leaf), never lifted to the top of the page
Mentions swr only as a last resort for data that must refresh client-side after initial load
notes: Guards the single most expensive default in this stack: reaching for a top-of-page 'use client' + useEffect to fetch data. A naive answer says 'yes, useEffect+fetch is standard React' — the skill knows the DeployNow rule is RSC server fetch, with the client boundary pushed to a small leaf.
